import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/opt/triposr')
import inspect
import torch
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
from tsr.system import TSR
from tsr.utils import get_rays

# Inspect what's available in tsr.utils for camera math
import tsr.utils as utils
print('=== Available in tsr.utils ===')
for name in dir(utils):
    obj = getattr(utils, name)
    if callable(obj) and not name.startswith('_'):
        print(name)

print('\n=== get_rays signature ===')
print(inspect.signature(get_rays))

device = 'cuda'
model = TSR.from_pretrained('/models/triposr', config_name='config.yaml', weight_name='model.ckpt')
model.renderer.set_chunk_size(131072)
model.to(device)

# Load a sample
import os, json
sample_obj = '0000ecca9a234cae994be239f6fec552'
obj_dir = os.path.join(os.environ.get('RAB_DATA_DIR','/data'), 'train', sample_obj)
input_img = Image.open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'input.png')).convert('RGB')
with open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'cameras.json')) as f:
    cameras = json.load(f)

# Encode
scene_codes = model([input_img], device=device)
print('Scene code shape:', type(scene_codes), len(scene_codes) if isinstance(scene_codes, list) else scene_codes.shape)

# Try to replicate get_spherical_cameras for custom pose
cam = cameras[1]  # first target view
azimuth = np.deg2rad(cam['azimuth'])
elevation = np.deg2rad(cam['elevation'])
distance = cam['distance']
fovy = np.deg2rad(49.1)
H, W = 256, 256

# Camera-to-world matrix (same as standard graphics)
# From spherical coords
x = distance * np.cos(elevation) * np.sin(azimuth)
y = distance * np.sin(elevation)
z = distance * np.cos(elevation) * np.cos(azimuth)
cam_pos = np.array([x, y, z])

# Look at origin
forward = -cam_pos / np.linalg.norm(cam_pos)
right = np.cross(forward, np.array([0, 1, 0]))
right = right / np.linalg.norm(right)
up = np.cross(right, forward)

R = np.stack([right, up, -forward], axis=0)  # 3x3
c2w = np.eye(4, dtype=np.float32)
c2w[:3, :3] = R
c2w[:3, 3] = cam_pos

c2w = torch.from_numpy(c2w).unsqueeze(0).to(device)

# Ray directions
focal_length = 0.5 * H / np.tan(0.5 * fovy)
i, j = torch.meshgrid(torch.linspace(0, W - 1, W), torch.linspace(0, H - 1, H), indexing='xy')
fx = fy = focal_length
cx, cy = 0.5 * W, 0.5 * H
directions = torch.stack([(i - cx) / fx, -(j - cy) / fy, -torch.ones_like(i)], dim=-1)  # [H, W, 3]
directions = directions.to(device)

rays_o, rays_d = get_rays(directions, c2w, keepdim=True, normalize=True)
print('rays_o shape:', rays_o.shape)
print('rays_d shape:', rays_d.shape)

# Render
with torch.no_grad():
    image = model.renderer(model.decoder, scene_codes[0], rays_o, rays_d)
print('Rendered image shape:', image.shape)
print('Rendered image min/max:', image.min().item(), image.max().item())

# Compare with default render
default_frames = model.render(scene_codes, n_views=1, elevation=cam['elevation'], camera_distance=cam['distance'], width=256, height=256, return_type='pt')
print('Default frame shape:', default_frames[0].shape if isinstance(default_frames, list) else default_frames.shape)
